title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 March 2025.

items.title: Japanese shrine aided by **Ghost of Tsushima** fans announces tourist ban due to "unforgivable act of a grave and unforgivable act of disrespect committed by foreigner(s)"
bullets: All tourists - domestic and foreign - are banned

items.title: **Game Informer** rescued by blockchain firm, with ‘entire team’ reunited
bullets: In an announcement posted on its revived website on Tuesday, Game Informer said it would be returning in both digital and print, with “the entire team” that was working together during its closure reunited.

items.title: Sims rival **InZOI** has become the most wishlisted game on Steam
bullets: As the game’s Early Access release on March 28 nears, it’s seen a surge in popularity, causing it to overtake the previous most wishlisted game, Hollow Knight: Silksong.
The game has also been followed on Steam by more than 50,000 followers in the past week, bringing its total to around 240,000.

items.title: Witcher and Cyberpunk’s **CD Projekt** is teaming up with ** Scopely** for an IP adaptation
bullets: The partnership was announced during CD Projekt‘s earnings call on Tuesday. No details were announced, nor was the specific IP that the game will use.
